pattern: Bitmask DP over the set of people already assigned: iterate hats 1-40 and for each hat assign it to any unassigned person who likes it.
hints: With at most 10 people, a bitmask of which people have been assigned a hat fits in an integer — use that as the DP state.
Iterate over hats (not people) as the outer dimension; for each hat, either skip it or assign it to some person who prefers it and is still unassigned.
dp[mask] = number of ways to fill the set of people encoded by mask using the hats seen so far; the answer is dp[(1<<n)-1] mod 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: one person who likes all 40 hats — 40 ways
10 people each liking all 40 hats — count permutations of 40 choose 10
two people share preference list — many reassignments possible
four people, each restricted to exactly 4 shared hats — 24 ways
two people with disjoint hat preferences — ways is product of preference sizes

stress.description: 10 people each with 10 random hat preferences
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    hats = []
    for _ in range(n):
        k = rng.randint(1, 10)
        prefs = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 41), k))
        hats.append(prefs)
    return [hats]
